items.title: Surgeon sounded 'exhausted' a week before fatal crash, brother says

children.content: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

children.content: Zabreena Manjerovic played state league Australian Rules football in Tasmania and after getting seven concussions in eight years her life changed forever.

items.title: Lawyers under pressure as court backlog hits 'crisis point'

items.title: Father dead, son hospitalised after crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: A man has died, and his son remains in hospital, following a single-vehicle crash in Blackmans Bay in Tasmania's south.

image.altText: A police and ambulance are parked across a residential street blocking the scene of a car crash.

items.title: LISTEN: How to get the most out of 'free power' hours

children.content: Tasmania's major energy retailer has been offering residential customers free electricity during specific time slots. So how can you make the most of it? An energy expert shares some tips.

items.title: Joshua pulled over to help with what he thought was car trouble. Then he saw the knife

children.content: When Tasmanian man Joshua Smith stopped on a South Australian highway late at night, his gut instinct told him something wasn't right. His act, which has been recognised with a bravery award, helped save the lives of two boys.

items.title: Treasurer 'willing to engage' on super for under-18s

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says he's open to discussing a guarantee for superannuation to be paid for teenage workers but stresses that's not the government's focus at the moment. Follow live.

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request government help as quake toll climbs

children.content: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

image.altText: an aerial image of apartment buildings reduced to rubble

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

image.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Multiple injuries, driver unaccounted for in truck-stop crash inferno

items.title: New fellowship for mid-career journalists honours Judith Whelan

children.content: The ABC, The Sydney Morning Herald and Sydney University have created a new fellowship to honour the late Judith Whelan, a trailblazing leader in media.

items.title: 'Systemic racism' tip of the iceberg in buried reef authority review

children.content: The damning results of a review into the agency tasked with managing the Great Barrier Reef, an area bigger than New Zealand and home to 70 traditional owner groups, has never before seen the light of day.

items.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

items.title: ASX trading sideways, Wall Street jumps as AI-related stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market has started its day relatively flat despite a rebound in oil prices ahead of the next round of US-Iran peace talks. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high. Follow live.

items.title: Sons remember 'loving' mum after Airbnb guest found guilty of killing her

items.title: Strait of Hormuz strikes likely to disrupt shipping again

items.title: Court rejects Trump's final bid to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

items.title: The medicine safety system most Australians have never heard of

children.content: A little black triangle on medicine information leaflets is meant to make it easier to report adverse effects of new medications. Problem is, many Australians don't know what it means.

items.title: Tourism operator says 'silence is deafening' about how to spot bird flu cases

items.title: Former England captain says Brook can't lead Test side under McCullum
